words.def: used before you mention something that makes a statement not completely true
words.example: I didn't tell him anything except that I needed the money.

words.def: an act of giving something to somebody or doing something for somebody and receiving something in return
words.example: The exchange of prisoners took place this morning.

words.def: the state of feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm
words.example: The news caused great excitement among her friends.

words.def: a collection of things, for example works of art, that are shown to the public
words.example: Have you seen the Picasso exhibition?

words.def: to become greater in size, number or importance; to make something greater in size, number or importance
words.example: Metals expand when they are heated.

words.def: the knowledge and skill that you have gained through doing something for a period of time; the process of gaining this
words.example: My lack of practical experience was a disadvantage.

words.def: having knowledge or skill in a particular job or activity
words.example: an experienced player/teacher

words.def: a scientific test that is done in order to study what happens and to gain new knowledge
words.example: to do/perform/conduct an experiment

words.def: to travel to or around an area or a country in order to learn about it
words.example: As soon as we arrived on the island we were eager to explore.

words.def: the sudden violent bursting and loud noise of something such as a bomb exploding; the act of deliberately causing something to explode
words.example: a bomb/nuclear/gas explosion

words.def: more than is usual, expected, or than exists already
words.example: extra money/cash/funding

words.def: the front part of the head, where the eyes, nose and mouth are
words.example: a pretty/pale/round face

words.def: to some extent but not very
words.example: fairly simple/easy/straightforward

words.def: an act of falling
words.example: I had a bad fall and broke my arm.

words.def: well known to you; often seen or heard and therefore easy to recognize
words.example: to look/sound/seem familiar

words.def: unusually complicated, often in an unnecessary way; intended to impress other people
words.example: a kitchen full of fancy gadgets

words.def: the business of managing or working on a farm
words.example: to take up farming

words.def: extremely interesting and attractive
words.example: a fascinating story

words.def: following a style that is popular at a particular time
words.example: fashionable clothes/ideas/styles

words.def: to close or join together the two parts of something; to become closed or joined together
words.example: fasten something, Fasten your seat belts, please.

words.def: the bad feeling that you have when you are in danger or when a particular thing frightens you
words.example: Her eyes showed no fear.

words.def: something important, interesting or typical of a place or thing
words.example: An interesting feature of the city is the old market.

words.def: a structure made of wood or wire supported with posts that is put between two areas of land as a boundary, or around a garden, field, etc. to keep animals in, or to keep people and animals out
words.example: The two women chatted over the garden fence.

words.def: 5th
words.example: Today is the fifth (of May).

words.def: connected with money and finance
words.example: the world's major financial markets/institutions

words.def: one of the four long thin parts that stick out from the hand (or five, if the thumb is included)
words.example: She ran her fingers through her hair.

words.def: the flames, light and heat, and often smoke, that are produced when something burns
words.example: Most animals are afraid of fire.

words.def: the state of being physically healthy and strong
words.example: a magazine on health and fitness

words.def: a piece of cloth with a special coloured design on it that may be the symbol of a particular country or organization, may be used to give a signal or may have a particular meaning. A flag can be attached to a pole (= a long thin straight piece of wood or metal) or held in the hand.
words.example: the Italian flag

words.def: having a level surface, not curved or sloping, and without holes or any bits sticking out
words.example: low buildings with flat roofs

words.def: a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry
words.example: The heavy rain has caused floods in many parts of the country.

words.def: a fine white or brown powder made from grain, especially wheat, and used in cooking for making bread, cakes, etc.
words.example: Sift the flour and salt into a bowl.

words.def: the steady and continuous movement of something/somebody in one direction
words.example: the flow of an electric current

words.def: a serious disease caused by a virus, that causes a high temperature, severe pains and weakness
words.example: The whole family has the flu.

words.def: traditional and typical of the ordinary people of a country or community
words.example: We visited an exhibition of folk art.

words.def: the lowest part of the leg, below the ankle, on which a person or an animal stands
words.example: My feet are aching.

words.def: violent physical action used to obtain or achieve something
words.example: The release of the hostages could not be achieved without the use of force.

words.def: used to say that a particular situation or state will always exist
words.example: I'll love you forever!

words.def: very correct and suitable for official or important occasions
words.example: ladies in formal evening wear

words.def: a strong border or structure of wood, metal, etc. that holds a picture, door, piece of glass, etc. in position
words.example: a picture/photo frame

words.def: to become hard, and often turn to ice, as a result of extreme cold; to make something do this
words.example: Water freezes at 0°C.

words.def: often
words.example: Buses run frequently between the city and the airport.

words.def: a relationship between friends
words.example: They formed a close friendship at college.

words.def: to make somebody suddenly feel afraid
words.example: frighten (somebody), Sorry, I didn't mean to frighten you.

words.def: afraid; feeling fear
words.example: a frightened child

words.def: making you feel afraid
words.example: a frightening experience/prospect/thought

words.def: kept at a very low temperature in order to preserve it
words.example: frozen peas/fish/pizza

words.def: to cook something in hot fat or oil; to be cooked in hot fat or oil
words.example: fry (something), fried fish

words.def: the soft thick mass of hair that grows on the body of some animals
words.example: The cat carefully licked its fur.

words.def: more; additional
words.example: Cook for a further 2 minutes.

words.def: taking place or existing at a time after the present
words.example: The land will remain untouched for future generations to enjoy.

words.def: a space where something is missing
words.example: Extra funding is needed to plug the gap.

words.def: a building for keeping one or more cars or other vehicles in
words.example: a double garage (= one for two cars)

words.def: to come together, or bring people together, in one place to form a group
words.example: A crowd soon gathered.

words.def: affecting all or most people, places or things
words.example: books of general interest (= of interest to most people)

words.def: by or to most people
words.example: The initiative was generally considered a success.

words.def: all the people who were born at about the same time
words.example: the younger/older generation

words.def: giving or willing to give freely; given freely
words.example: The gallery was named after its most generous benefactor.

words.def: calm and kind; doing things in a quiet and careful way
words.example: a kind and gentle man

words.def: a man who is polite and well educated, who has excellent manners and always behaves well
words.example: You acted like a true gentleman.

words.def: the scientific study of the earth’s surface, physical features, divisions, products, population, etc.
words.example: recent work in economic geography

words.def: the spirit of a dead person that a living person believes they can see or hear
words.example: Do you believe in ghosts (= believe that they exist)?

words.def: very large; much larger or more important than similar things usually are
words.example: The match was shown on a giant screen outside the town hall.
